2604.13705.title: Beyond Arrow's Impossibility: Fairness as an Emergent Property of Multi-Agent Collaboration
2604.13705.paper_summary: Fairness in language models is typically studied as a property of a single, centrally optimized model. As large language models become increasingly agentic, we propose that fairness emerges through interaction and exchange. We study this via a controlled hospital triage framework in which two agents negotiate over three structured debate rounds. One agent is aligned to a specific ethical framework via retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), while the other is either unaligned or adversarially prompted to favor demographic groups over clinical need. We find that alignment systematically shapes negotiation strategies and allocation patterns, and that neither agent's allocation is ethically adequate in isolation, yet their joint final allocation can satisfy fairness criteria that neither would have reached alone. Aligned agents partially moderate bias through contestation rather than override, acting as corrective patches that restore access for marginalized groups without fully converting a biased counterpart. We further observe that even explicitly aligned agents exhibit intrinsic biases toward certain frameworks, consistent with known left-leaning tendencies in LLMs. We connect these limits to Arrow's Impossibility Theorem: no aggregation mechanism can simultaneously satisfy all desiderata of collective rationality, and multi-agent deliberation navigates rather than resolves this constraint. Our results reposition fairness as an emergent, procedural property of decentralized agent interaction, and the system rather than the individual agent as the appropriate unit of evaluation.
2604.13705.paper_summary_zh: 公平性在語言模型中通常被視為單一、集中優化模型的特性。隨著大型語言模型變得越來越具代理性，我們提出公平性是通過互動和交流而出現的。我們通過一個受控的醫院分診框架來研究這一點，在這個框架中，兩個代理在三輪結構化辯論中進行協商。一個代理通過檢索增強生成（RAG）與特定的倫理框架對齊，而另一個則是未對齊或被對抗性地提示以偏向於人口群體而非臨床需求。我們發現，對齊系統性地塑造了協商策略和分配模式，且無論哪一個代理的分配在孤立情況下都不符合倫理標準，但他們的聯合最終分配可以滿足公平性標準，而這是任何一方單獨都無法達到的。對齊的代理通過競爭而非覆蓋部分地調節偏見，作為修正性補丁，恢復邊緣化群體的訪問權，而不完全轉變偏見的對手。我們進一步觀察到，即使是明確對齊的代理也對某些框架表現出內在偏見，這與大型語言模型中已知的左傾趨勢一致。我們將這些限制與阿羅不可能性定理聯繫起來：沒有任何聚合機制可以同時滿足集體理性的所有期望，而多代理的審議是導航而非解決這一約束。我們的結果重新定位公平性為一種新興的、程序性的特性，源於去中心化的代理互動，系統而非個別代理是適當的評估單位。
2604.13705.author: Sayan Kumar Chaki et.al.
2604.13705.authors: Sayan Kumar Chaki, Antoine Gourru, Julien Velcin
